Compatibility alias for the canonical `content_queue` skill.


# Content Queue Manager

Manage the 14-day content publishing calendar stored at `~/hairglasses-studio/docs/strategy/launch-content/content-calendar.json`.

## State file

`~/hairglasses-studio/docs/strategy/launch-content/content-calendar.json` — JSON with `marathon_start`, `marathon_end`, and `items[]` array. Each item has: `day`, `date`, `slot` (morning/evening), `item_number`, `title`, `platform`, `source_file`, `status` (ready/drafted/posted/skipped), `effort` (R/E/W), `est_minutes`, `posted_url`, `posted_at`.

## Default loop

1. Read `content-calendar.json`. If absent, report "No calendar initialized. Run `/content-marathon start` to create one."

2. Parse `$ARGUMENTS`:

   **`status`** (default if no args):
   - Count items by status (ready/drafted/posted/skipped)
   - Show today's items with their status
   - Calculate: days elapsed, days remaining, posts on track vs behind
   - Display a compact progress bar

   **`review`**:
   - List all items marked "posted" with their URLs
   - Check gist URLs are still live: `gh gist list --limit 20`
   - Check awesome-list PR statuses: `gh pr list --author hairglasses --state all`
   - Report any posted items missing a URL

   **`next`**:
   - Show the next 2-4 unpublished items in chronological order
   - Include: title, platform, effort level, estimated minutes, source file path
   - Flag if any are overdue (date < today and status != posted)

   **`calendar`**:
   - Display the full 14-day calendar as a markdown table
   - Color-code by status: posted, drafted, ready, skipped, overdue

   **`mark <item#> posted <url>`**:
   - Set item status to "posted", record URL and timestamp
   - Commit updated calendar to docs repo

   **`skip <item#> <reason>`**:
   - Set item status to "skipped" with reason
   - Commit updated calendar

   **`reschedule <item#> <new_day>`**:
   - Move item to a different day, swap if needed
   - Commit updated calendar

3. After any mutation, commit `content-calendar.json` to the docs repo:
   ```bash
   cd ~/hairglasses-studio/docs && git add strategy/launch-content/content-calendar.json && git commit -m "content: update calendar state" && git push origin main
   ```

## Notes

- All dates are in local time (PDT/America/Los_Angeles)
- The calendar JSON is the single source of truth for marathon state
- Never modify content files from this skill — that's content_writer's job
